name: Colomera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Colomera — circular desde Cauro
description: This 20.1-mile mountain biking route near Cauro offers moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and scenic views of Embalse de Colomera.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Colomera offers diverse landscapes and varied topography, making it a notable destination for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by mountains, valleys, and significant water bodies, providing a range of terrain for mountain bikers. Trails often lead through scrubland and olive groves, with routes offering views of the Colomera Reservoir and the distant Sierra Nevada mountains. This area provides both scenic and challenging terrain for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Colomera The most popular mountain bike trail is Colomera Reservoir Loop, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers spectacular views around the central Colomera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Gollizno Route Trail, a moderate 11.2 miles (18.0 km) path. This route explores the Velillos River canyon, providing a blend of natural exploration and varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Urbanización Los Cortijos 2, a 25.0 miles (40.2 km) trail leading through the Cubillas Reservoir area, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Colomera is defined by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and significant reservoirs, alongside olive groves and scrubland. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Colomera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Colomera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Colomera?
A: Colomera offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes available. These range from easy paths to more challenging ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Colomera?
A: The trails in Colomera are quite diverse. You'll find 2 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for both beginners and experienced riders looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Colomera?
A: Yes, Colomera features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017567920/colomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 32.4 km trail offering spectacular views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Urbanización Los Cortijos 2&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.2 km long and also moderate.

Q: What are the best trails for beginners or families in Colomera?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are 2 easy-rated trails available for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically offer gentler terrain and shorter distances, perfect for families or beginners.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Colomera?
A: Mountain biking in Colomera offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sights. You can encounter landmarks like the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinos Puente Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the adventurous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that explore river canyons or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its significant water bodies and varied topography. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030998844/gollizno-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores the Velillos River canyon, offering a unique blend of natural exploration. Many routes also circle the Colomera and Cubillas Reservoirs, providing scenic views.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often lead through scrubland and olive groves, providing panoramic views. You can expect spectacular vistas of the Colomera Reservoir and village, and from higher points, even glimpses of the distant Sierra Nevada mountains.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Colomera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the varied topography, and the combination of challenging and scenic routes that offer both adventure and cultural immersion.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes in Colomera?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 11 difficult-rated routes. One notable difficult trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Fuente Vieja (Old Fountain) loop from Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 70.8 km and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Colomera?
A: Colomera's climate makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are mild but can have cooler days.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in Colomera?
A: Trail distances vary significantly. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017567920/colomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; are around 32 km and take about 3 hours. Longer, more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Fuente Vieja (Old Fountain) loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be over 70 km and take more than 5 hours to complete.
tips: The top presents excellent views of the Colomera reservoir hidden behind the mountains.

Located on the Colomera y de las Juntas riverbed, the Colomera reservoir was built between 1982 and 1989. Since its inception, the reservoir has been used to produce electricity, supply water to the towns in the area and the irrigation of the fields.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Embalse de Colomera — circular desde Cauro mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered to be of moderate to hard difficulty, suitable for riders with good to very good fitness levels. While much of it consists of good tracks and asphalt, there are challenging sections, including steep singletrack.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll find mostly well-maintained tracks (60%) and asphalt roads (30%), along with some unpaved sections and singletrack trails (10%). Be prepared for a mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there any sections where I might need to dismount and push my bike?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a particularly steep uphill singletrack segment where cyclists may need to dismount and push their bikes. Additionally, a connecting path to the Montillana road is reportedly impassable by bike, also requiring riders to walk.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the Colomera Reservoir and the surrounding countryside. The route passes through extensive agricultural lands, primarily olive groves, and you'll see traditional farmhouses (cortijos). Panoramic vistas of the valley and the reservoir are available from points like Fuente Cauro.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Key points include the Colomera Reservoir itself, and a notable view of Colomera Reservoir from Puerto de los Lobos Harteros. You'll also encounter the Colomera river, the Fuente de las Monjas spring, and potentially historical sites like the Cortijo de Elvira.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Colomera — circular desde Cauro trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for encountering wildlife or agricultural areas. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the Granada province of Spain. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain biking route.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Colomera — circular desde Cauro trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends circular desde Cauro. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or near the village of Cauro to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any places to rest or refill water along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Fuente de las Monjas is a spring with powerful water spouts that serves as a good rest stop. It's always recommended to carry sufficient water for the duration of your ride, especially on a route of this length and difficulty.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

waypoints.name: View of Colomera Reservoir from Puerto de los Lobos Harteros
